title: I built a Telegram AI agent that trades crypto, writes code, and manages servers — tested DeFi with $5 in SOL, woke up to +37%
author: adambaialiev

replies.author: adambaialiev
replies.body:
I just had a chat with the agent. It is smart to create its own server, asked my codex subscription, did research, and it wrote a bunch of scripts to do swaps and here we go. 

@AgenticCloudComputerBot in Telegram

comments.author: Obviously_not_maayan
comments.body: You connected openclaw to a telegram channel, chill

replies.author: adambaialiev
replies.body: OpenClaw is a local terminal tool you self-host — you bring your own keys and figure out infra yourself. This thing provisions cloud servers, launches parallel terminal sessions, and executes whatever you ask from chat. The SOL swap was the agent running commands on a server it spun up. Looks similar on the outside, completely different on the inside.

comments.author: dwa_jz
comments.body: One night is not proving anything though

replies.author: adambaialiev
replies.body: Yeah fair, sample size of one lol. Just thought the workflow was cool — bot spawns a server, writes the scripts, does the thing. That part works every time.
